# Pagesmith

> Filesystem-first content toolkit organized as a multi-package workspace under the `@pagesmith/` npm scope.

## Summary

- Two main packages: `@pagesmith/core` for the shared content/runtime layer and `@pagesmith/docs` for convention-based documentation with built-in Pagefind search
- Typed filesystem collections for markdown and structured data (JSON, JSONC, JSON5, YAML, TOML)
- Lazy markdown rendering with Expressive Code syntax highlighting (dual themes, line numbers, file titles, mark/ins/del, collapse, copy button), headings, and read-time metadata
- GitHub Flavored Markdown, GitHub Alerts, math (LaTeX/MathJax), smart typography, accessible emojis, and auto-linked headings
- Vite-native: content plugin (`pagesmithContent`) and SSG plugin (`pagesmithSsg`) for Vite
- Schema validation with Zod at the boundary, plus AST-level content validators (links, headings, code blocks)
- JSX runtime for server-side HTML generation (framework-agnostic)
- Docs-site defaults from `pagesmith.config.json5`, with optional layout overrides through `theme.layouts.*`
- Bundled Pagefind search with Cmd+K keyboard shortcut
- Assistant artifact helpers exposed from `@pagesmith/core/ai`

## Quick Start

For a docs site with defaults:

```bash
npm add @pagesmith/docs
```

```json5
{
  name: 'Acme Docs',
  title: 'Acme Docs',
  description: 'Team documentation',
  contentDir: './content',
  search: { enabled: true },
}
```

For a custom site or framework integration:

```bash
npm add @pagesmith/core
```

```ts
import { createContentLayer, defineCollection, defineConfig, z } from '@pagesmith/core'

const posts = defineCollection({
  loader: 'markdown',
  directory: 'content/posts',
  schema: z.object({ title: z.string(), date: z.coerce.date() }),
})

const layer = createContentLayer(defineConfig({ collections: { posts } }))
const entries = await layer.getCollection('posts')
const rendered = await entries[0]?.render()
// rendered.html, rendered.headings, rendered.readTime
```

Use `@pagesmith/docs` when you want the built-in docs experience. Use `@pagesmith/core` plus `@pagesmith/core/vite` when you need custom layouts or framework-specific rendering (React, Solid, Svelte, EJS, Handlebars).

## Framework Support

Vite-based integration with any framework through two patterns:
- **Virtual modules** (`pagesmithContent` plugin): `virtual:content/posts` — for React, Solid, Svelte
- **Programmatic API** (`createContentLayer`): for EJS, Handlebars, and custom setups
- **SSG** (`pagesmithSsg` plugin): static site generation with dev server + Pagefind search

## Docs Content Structure

Content follows a folder convention for `@pagesmith/docs`:
- `content/README.md` — home page
- Top-level folders → navigation sections and sidebar groups
- Subfolders with `README.md` → individual pages
- `meta.json5` files → section ordering, series grouping, display names
- Frontmatter: title, description, navLabel, sidebarLabel, order, draft (all optional)
